Haki Profile


## Rime's Haki Mastery



Rime possesses all three forms of Haki — a testament to the depth of his will despite his small body. His Armament Haki is his crown jewel, refined through his Wano lineage into the most advanced Ryou mastery on record. His Observation and Conqueror's Haki are formidable in their own right, both at a level that would make seasoned New World veterans take notice.







Armament Haki — Ryou

Emperor

Awakened Internal Destruction




Fully awakened Ryou capable of projecting destructive force through any medium at any depth. Targets suffer internal devastation from a single touch. Among the highest recorded Armament mastery in the world.






Observation Haki

Elite Officer

Enhanced Perception + Intent Reading




Acute awareness of hostile intent, battlefield positioning, and incoming attacks. Not future-sight grade, but highly refined threat detection capable of reading complex multi-target scenarios.






Conqueror's Haki

Elite Officer

Targeted Suppression + Will Imposition




Capable of overwhelming weaker-willed opponents and exerting focused spiritual pressure in close quarters. Not broad-range domination, but devastatingly effective in direct confrontation. His Conqueror's manifests as a choking heaviness — opponents feel the air turn caustic and dense, their will corroding before they understand why.









Martial Arts


## Rokushiki — Specialist Adaptation



Rime has learned Rokushiki at a proficient specialist level, adapted entirely for his chinchilla body — explosive vertical leaps, compressible soft fur, and a long powerful tail that most practitioners lack.







Soru


Chinchillas can leap six feet vertically from a standstill. Rime's Soru is built on this — explosive lateral and vertical bursts that leave a puff of mineral dust in his wake. Over short distances, he is functionally invisible.






Geppo


Rapid-fire air-jumps trailing wisps of acidic dust cloud at each kick-off point. His light weight means each step is nearly instantaneous. He can chain Geppo indefinitely, leaving a hanging trail of corrosive particulates that punishes anyone who follows his path.






Tekkai


Compressed mineral dust + Ryou armour. Rime saturates his fur with volcanic dust and hardens the layer with Armament projection — his soft chinchilla body suddenly looks and feels like stone. The contrast is terrifying. Striking dust-Tekkai Rime earns an acid burn, an Electro discharge, and Ryou internal damage simultaneously.






Shigan


Twig-thrust variants — Rime's kendō sword becomes a piercing lance channeled with Ryou. Also: chinchilla teeth are incredibly hard. His bite-based Shigan is devastating at point-blank range, delivering Electro directly through enamel into the target.






Rankyaku


Twig slashes that launch arcs of acidic vapour. Also: tail-whip Rankyaku from his long, powerful chinchilla tail — a compressed air blade trailing caustic mist that most opponents do not see coming from a fluffy creature's hindquarters.






Kami-e


Chinchilla agility is absurd — they slip through impossibly small gaps. Rime's Kami-e leverages his soft, compressible body + Logia vapour dissolution to become nearly impossible to hit. He flows around attacks like smoke, reforming behind the strike with his twig already mid-swing.









Combat Doctrine


## Rime's Combat Doctrine



Rime's combat style is built around a single terrifying principle: nothing he touches survives the contact unchanged. He fights by closing distance through Soru and Geppo micro-bursts, landing on or near opponents, and delivering vapour-Electro-Ryou strikes through any point of contact — paws, twig sword, teeth, even the soles of his feet as he air-steps off an opponent's body. His vapour runs hot, acidic, and alkaline — superheated corrosive mist that eats through armour and blisters skin, modulated with terrifying precision.




His Logia intangibility makes him nearly impossible to hit without Haki, and even Haki users must contend with the fact that striking Rime's vapour body triggers Electro discharge and acid burns on contact. He fights dirty, fast, and with complete disregard for the dignity of the encounter — scurrying up limbs, biting ears while channeling Ryou, melting weapons mid-swing with superheated steam, swinging his twig sword in devastating Ryou-channeled arcs, and chattering incessantly the entire time. His fur carries a permanent charge of volcanic mineral dust from his dust baths — in combat, this dust billows out in abrasive clouds that get into armour joints, eyes, and mechanisms.




Against serious opponents, Rime shifts to area-control warfare: spreading acidic vapour fields laden with electrified mineral dust to dominate the terrain, pelting targets with Ryou-compressed grain rounds from unpredictable angles, adjusting the pH of his vapour to corrode armor and dissolve organic barriers, and reserving his most devastating Ryou strikes for decisive moments. His preferred finisher is always the same — one paw, one touch, total internal collapse. The gap between his adorable appearance and his destructive output is itself a psychological weapon. No one prepares adequately for a pudgy chinchilla with a twig sword that hits harder than a battleship cannon.





Logia


## Jōki Jōki no Mi — Vapour-Vapour Fruit



The Jōki Jōki no Mi is a Logia-type Devil Fruit that allows the user to create, control, and become vapour — the gaseous transitional state of water. On the spectrum of water-element Logias, it sits as the counterpart to wind fruits, much as smoke complements gas and fire complements lava. On paper, it seems weak: vapour lacks ice's solidity, snow's volume, and water's mass. It dissipates quickly and carries limited force.




What makes the Jōki Jōki no Mi extraordinary is its plasma intensity and chemical versatility. Rime produces superheated steam — capable of inflicting severe burns, melting metal, and creating pressure explosions. He can adjust the pH of his vapour, producing acidic mist that corrodes armour and dissolves organic barriers, or alkaline fog that neutralizes chemical threats and strips protective coatings. The vapour also serves as a carrier medium for his volcanic mineral dust — suspended particulates that abrade, blind, and infiltrate every gap in an opponent's defenses.




But the truly terrifying interaction is with Electro. When electrical charge is passed through superheated steam, the energy ionizes the vapour into plasma — a state of matter hotter than fire, more conductive than metal, and more destructive than almost any natural element. Rime's plasma-state attacks glow with a searing crimson-white luminance and carry devastation far beyond what either Electro or steam could achieve alone. When mineral dust is suspended in the ionized vapour, each particle becomes a micro-lightning conductor — arcing electricity between dust motes in a visible web of crackling energy. The "weakness" of the fruit becomes its greatest asset — vapour's instability makes it infinitely adaptable.





Mink Heritage


## Electro — Anomalous Plasma State



As a Mink hybrid, Rime inherited Electro — the natural ability of the Mink tribe to generate and channel electrical energy through their fur. In most Minks, Electro manifests as crackling sparks that enhance physical strikes. In Rime, it became something else entirely. His Electro interacts anomalously with his Vapour Logia, and the result is plasma — ionized gas hotter than fire, more conductive than metal, more destructive than almost any natural element.




The interaction is bidirectional: Electro supercharges his vapour into plasma states, while his Logia's vapour control allows him to modulate Electro output with surgical precision. Acidic vapour saturated with Electro creates an ionized corrosive field — metal armour corrodes twice as fast, nervous systems overload from the combination of chemical and electrical attack. When mineral dust enters the equation, each charged particle arcs micro-lightning to its neighbours, creating the Gremlin Field — Rime's ultimate area-denial state: a sphere of mineral dust suspended in acidic vapour, all of it crackling with Electro. Inside the Gremlin Field, visibility is zero, every breath is acid, every surface is sandpaper, and lightning strikes from every direction. Rime sits in the centre, perfectly comfortable (Logia immunity + dust bath), cackling. This acid-electric-dust synergy is what elevates Rime from a competent Logia user to one of the most dangerous combatants in the New World — a living fusion reactor wrapped in chinchilla fur.



Wano Heritage


## Awakened Ryou



The Mink DNA used in Rime's creation was sourced from a Wano-lineage Mink — and with it came an innate affinity for Ryou, the Wano tradition of advanced Armament Haki that projects force beyond the point of contact. Rime didn't just inherit the affinity. He mastered it to the absolute pinnacle.




Rime's Ryou has reached full awakened mastery — the ability to project devastating internal destruction through any surface, at any range his tiny paws can touch. A chinchilla paw-tap backed by awakened Ryou can shatter steel from the inside. Combined with his vapour and Electro, Rime can deliver acid-electric Ryou strikes that corrode, electrocute, and rupture internal structures simultaneously. Channeled through his twig sword, Ryou extends the blade's reach with invisible force projection — a stick becomes a weapon that cuts through anything, because the cutting isn't physical. The absurdity of a pudgy chinchilla possessing this level of Haki mastery is itself a kind of weapon — no one sees it coming.





Named Techniques


## Rime's Signature Moves







Vapour Art — Acid

Corrosion Palm


▾






蒸術・酸式・腐蝕掌 — Jōjutsu Sanshiki: Fushoku Shō


Rime places a single paw on any surface and channels awakened Ryou through an acid-vapour-Electro lattice directly into the target's internal structure. Superheated acidic vapour permeates the target at the molecular level while electrical discharge disrupts the nervous system. Against living targets: paralysis and chemical burns from the inside out. Against materials: internal acid corrosion causes catastrophic structural failure — steel dissolves like sugar. The most precise technique in Rime's acid arsenal, disguised as nothing more than a gentle touch.











Vapour Art — Dust

Sandstorm Veil


▾






蒸術・塵式・砂嵐幕 — Jōjutsu Jinshiki: Sunaarashi Maku


Rime disperses acidic vapour laden with volcanic mineral dust across the battlefield as a creeping storm of electrified abrasive haze. The dust-acid combination coats every surface it touches — grinding into armour joints, clogging mechanisms, and etching exposed metal with corrosive particulates. Opponents caught in the veil find their movements impaired by dust infiltration and periodically jolted by ambient Electro discharge arcing between charged dust particles. The veil doubles as a sensory tool: Rime feels pressure changes and vibrations through the dust density, giving him tremor-sense across the entire hazard zone. The longer an opponent stays, the worse it gets — dust accumulates in every crevice, acid eats through every seal, and the static charge builds until the air itself crackles.











Twig Sword Art

Heaven-Splitting Flash


▾






天裂一閃 — Tenretsu Issen


A single vertical slash from Rime's twig — Ryou channeled through the entire length of the stick, extending the cutting edge with invisible projected force far beyond the twig's physical reach. The slash releases an arc of acidic vapour that hangs in the air for a heartbeat before detonating. The technique name suggests a legendary swordsman's finishing move. The reality is a chinchilla swinging a twig so fast that the air screams. Both descriptions are accurate. The slash has cut clean through Marine-grade steel plating. The twig was undamaged. Rime was insufferable about it for days.











Vapour Art — Hot Mode

Scalding Miasma


▾






蒸術・熱式・灼熱瘴気 — Jōjutsu Neshiki: Shakunetsu Shōki


Rime disperses into his Logia state, spreading across the battlefield as a creeping bank of superheated acidic steam. The pH-adjusted vapour corrodes exposed metal, blisters skin, and turns the air itself into a scalding hazard. Opponents caught in the miasma find their vision obscured, their lungs burning, and their armor degrading. The steam is also a sensory tool: Rime feels pressure changes and movement through the vapour density, effectively giving him total spatial awareness across the entire fog. He can selectively intensify heat in specific zones, creating invisible kill-pockets within the seemingly uniform haze.











Electro-Vapour — Plasma

Plasma Geyser — Crimson Pillar


▾






雷蒸・紅柱噴出 — Raijō: Kōchū Funshutsu


Rime generates a concentrated column of superheated steam from his position and then floods it with maximum Electro output. The electrical charge ionizes the steam into plasma — a searing crimson-white pillar of ionized gas hotter than any natural fire. The column erupts upward like a volcanic geyser, incinerating everything in its path. Air around the pillar shimmers and warps. The ground beneath blackens and vitrifies. This is Rime's primary area-denial tool and his most visually terrifying technique — a roaring column of plasma erupting from a tiny chinchilla. Beautiful from a very long distance. Instantly lethal up close.











Electro-Vapour

Plasma Seed


▾






雷蒸・紅核種弾 — Raijō: Kōkaku Tanedan


Rime's signature projectile. He compresses superheated steam and Electro into a tiny seed-shaped pellet between his paws — the vapour ionizing into a dense plasma core barely contained by electromagnetic pressure. He flicks it at the target with surprising accuracy. On impact, the containment fails and the seed detonates into a localized plasma explosion — a searing burst of ionized gas and electrical discharge that melts, burns, and electrocutes simultaneously. The projectile is small enough to be nearly invisible in flight and packs enough force to crater stone. Rime can produce these rapidly, chattering happily while pelting opponents with what amount to tiny stellar grenades.











Ryou Art

Wano's Smallest Fist


▾






流桜術・和の最小拳 — Ryūō-jutsu: Wa no Saishō Ken


Pure Ryou, no vapour, no Electro. Rime channels the full depth of his awakened Armament into a single paw strike. The projection force travels through any medium — flesh, armor, sea-stone, earth — and detonates internally at whatever depth Rime chooses. The technique's name is its own joke: the smallest fist in Wano, carrying destruction that would make samurai generals weep. Rime typically uses this when he wants to make a point. One tap. Total devastation. Then he goes back to eating nuts.











Electro-Vapour

Vapour Cage — Boiling Lattice


▾






雷蒸・沸騰格子 — Raijō: Futtō Kōshi


Rime constructs a geometric cage of interlocking vapour walls around the target — alternating between superheated acidic steam barriers and dense mineral-dust panels in a hexagonal lattice pattern. The differential makes the cage self-reinforcing: hot acidic vapour rises against the heavier dust-laden walls, creating convection currents that maintain structural integrity and continuously circulate abrasive particulates. Each wall is threaded with dormant Electro. Any contact triggers a full discharge. Attempting to break through the steam panels causes severe acid burns; breaking through the dust panels triggers a sandblasting burst that strips skin and corrodes armour. And Rime can collapse the entire cage inward on command, compressing the vapour into a superheated implosion that transitions to plasma at the convergence point. A prison that punishes escape in every direction.











Ultimate — Gremlin Field

The Gremlin's Domain


▾






蒸術・塵酸奥義・小鬼の領域 — Jōjutsu Jinsan Ōgi: Kooni no Ryōiki


Rime's area-denial ultimate. He plants both paws on the ground and channels awakened Ryou, maximum Electro output, and full vapour generation into a single omnidirectional expansion — acidic vapour saturated with volcanic mineral dust erupting outward in a visible ring. The wave coats every surface in corrosive dust. Lightning arcs between charged particles in a web of crackling destruction. The air turns caustic. The ground becomes abrasive sandpaper. Every breath burns. Every surface strips. Every metal corrodes. For anyone caught inside the Gremlin's Domain, visibility is zero, every inhale is acid, every contact is sandblasting, and electricity strikes from every direction. Rime sits at the centre of the sphere, perfectly comfortable — Logia immunity negates the acid, dust bath instinct makes the mineral cloud feel like home, and Electro is his own element. He cackles. Less destructive than Thermal Collapse, but faster to deploy and with a wider area of effect — Rime's answer when he needs to shut down an entire engagement zone instantly rather than obliterate it.











Ultimate — Dual Mode

Thermal Collapse — Rime's Verdict


▾






熱崩壊・霜の裁き — Netsu Hōkai: Rime no Sabaki


Rime's most powerful technique. He plants both paws on the ground, drives his twig sword into the earth beside him, and channels everything — awakened Ryou, maximum Electro output, full Logia vapour generation, and every grain of mineral dust in his reserves simultaneously. First, a wave of superheated acidic vapour saturated with electrified dust expands outward, corroding all surfaces and charging the air with lethal static. Then, a heartbeat later, the twig sword acts as a lightning rod — every particle of charged dust in the field arcs back toward it, and the convergence detonates into a pillar of plasma that erupts from the sword's position outward. The chemical-electric shock is catastrophic — corroded materials shatter explosively, the air itself ruptures, and everything in range is subjected to simultaneous acid dissolution, dust abrasion, and plasma incineration. The battlefield becomes a hellscape of caustic fog and lightning, every surface stripped raw. For a brief moment, Rime commands every element in his arsenal at once. Then he passes out from exhaustion and needs to be carried in Ajay's coat pocket for the next several hours.











Resonance Art — Embers

Ember Swarm — Chain Ignition Cascade


▾






燼共鳴術・連鎖点火滝 — Jin Kyōmei-jutsu: Rensa Tenka Taki


Rime calls the Embers to him and floods their tiny spark-cores with raw Logia vapour — superheating every one of them until they glow white-hot. Then he launches them. The Embers scatter across the battlefield like a volley of living incendiary rounds, each one trailing a ribbon of superheated vapour. When the first Ember detonates on contact, it ignites the vapour trail — which connects to the next Ember, which detonates and ignites its trail — creating a cascading chain-ignition that races across the entire network in seconds. The result is a web of plasma-fire that maps perfectly to wherever the Embers flew, turning Rime's scattered little soot-sprites into a precision-targeted firestorm. Hinoko always volunteers to be the first Ember launched. Rime always lets her. The Embers reform after the flames die down, slightly singed, absolutely ecstatic, already asking to do it again.








The Twig


## Rime's Sword



Rime carries a twig. A literal twig — a small stick from a koa tree, found on a winter island during the crew's third month together. He picked it up, swung it twice, declared it "the greatest sword on the Grand Line," and has carried it ever since. It sits in a tiny scabbard on his bandolier that Tidus built for him without being asked. The twig is approximately twelve centimetres long, slightly curved, and unremarkable in every physical sense. Rime treats it like a legendary blade — he polishes it, he talks to it, he has named it (he will not share the name), and he performs formal kendō kata with it every morning in the Crucible while Gauge watches in what can only be described as quiet concern.




The comedy: it is a twig. The reality: channeled with awakened Ryou, the twig's cutting edge extends far beyond its physical length through projected force. Electro arcs along the wood grain. Acidic vapour trails every slash. A chinchilla performing perfect kendō forms with a stick is one of the most dangerous swordsmanship displays in the New World, and the gap between the visual and the output is exactly the kind of thing that makes Rime insufferably happy.





Kenjutsu Mastery


## The Chinchilla Who Reinvented the Blade



Rime's swordsmanship is not an imitation of human kenjutsu. It is something entirely new — a philosophy of the blade built from the ground up by a chinchilla who decided the entire history of swordsmanship needed revision and revised it alone in a room lined with acid-scarred walls. His kenjutsu integrates acid-vapour feints, Electro-accelerated slashes, mineral-dust blinds, and Ryou-projected cutting force in combinations no human sword school has ever conceived. Where a human swordsman relies on reach, leverage, and muscle memory, Rime relies on chemical warfare, electrical augmentation, and a Ryou mastery so profound that the twig's cutting edge extends indefinitely through projected force alone.




The result is a swordsmanship that matches the greatest blades in the world. During the engagement against the Red Hair Pirates, Rime parried "Red-Haired" Shanks' Divine Departure — a Conqueror's-coated sword stroke from an Emperor — with his twig. The clash produced a Conqueror's shockwave visible from orbit. For three exchanges, Shanks could not get past him. The man whose swordsmanship is whispered alongside Mihawk's fought a chinchilla with a stick and found a peer, not because the weapons were equal but because Rime's understanding of what a blade could be exceeded anything conventional swordsmanship had prepared Shanks for. Acid trails that punished every parry. Electro-accelerated ripostes faster than muscle memory could counter. Mineral-dust clouds that blinded between slashes. And underneath all of it, Ryou — the deepest, most refined Armament projection on the planet — turning a twelve-centimetre twig into an infinite cutting edge.




Rime teaches his kenjutsu to the Vanguard during training sessions in the Crucible — sessions that started as play and became something extraordinary. The Twelve Who Stand learned to channel Ryou through their tiny weapons with the same philosophy that makes a twig the equal of a legendary blade. Harui's sewing needle. Gankaku's pillow-shield edge. Kozuchi's oversized mallet. All of them became something more under Rime's instruction — not conventional sword techniques adapted for small bodies, but an entirely new martial tradition built by small bodies for small bodies. Rime calls it nothing. He refuses to name his school. But Claude has logged it as the Crucible Tradition, and the Vanguard carry it into every battle.





The Grain Arsenal


## Weaponized Hoarding



Rime hoards grain — oats, barley, millet, and whatever cereal he can stockpile with obsessive chinchilla dedication. This is both instinct and deliberate tactical choice. He has modified and infused grain rounds with different effects for battle use. He keeps them in pouches on his tiny bandolier and in hidden stashes all over the Meridian that he thinks nobody knows about (Claude knows about all of them). In combat, he launches them through vapour compression at terrifying velocity, flicks them off the flat of his twig sword with Ryou hardening, or paw-flicks them with Electro charge — his small size means opponents underestimate the lethality of a pudgy chinchilla throwing breakfast cereal until it's far too late. He takes his grain selection as seriously as a sommelier takes wine.







Vapour Rounds


Grain kernels compressed in superheated acidic vapour and launched at high velocity. Explode into scalding corrosive steam clouds on impact. His most common combat projectile. He carries at least thirty at all times.






Electro Rounds


Charged with his Mink Electro. Discharge on contact like miniature lightning grenades. The electrical burst can stun, paralyze, or — at full charge — stop a heart. Rime limits the charge for non-lethal engagements. He does not always limit the charge.






Smoke Rounds


Produce thick concealment fog. Useful for retreats, repositioning, and giving Petal's Observation an advantage while blinding enemy visual tracking. The fog smells faintly of toasted oats, which Rime finds hilarious and everyone else finds oddly comforting in a crisis.






Signal Rounds


Modified by Tidus. Emit a high-pitched frequency that summons the Embers to Rime's location. Used as a rally call. The Embers arrive like a tiny cavalry, disorganized and enthusiastic, which is somehow exactly what Rime needs.






Null Rounds


Rare, difficult to make. Temporarily suppress Devil Fruit abilities in a small radius on detonation. Reverse-engineered from Seastone dust Tidus procured through channels he won't discuss. The suppression lasts only seconds, but in a fight between Devil Fruit users, seconds are lifetimes.






Grain Mines


Scattered across the battlefield, each grain infused with Electro, creating a minefield of tiny explosions. Step on one and the static discharge chains to every other grain in a two-metre radius. A field of breakfast cereal that detonates like a carpet of micro-grenades. Rime considers this his most elegant invention.






Lucky Grain


One golden oat kernel he never throws. He's had it since the lab. He doesn't talk about it. Petal knows what it means. Ajay suspects. It sits in a tiny velvet pouch in the innermost pocket of his bandolier, and when things get truly bad — when the fight seems impossible — Rime touches it once through the fabric, and then he fights like the impossible doesn't apply to him.









The Meridian's Lifeline


## Rime's Non-Combat Roles



For all his combat bravado, Rime performs three critical functions aboard the Meridian that make the ship's autonomous operation possible. Without him, the Meridian would need to dock at ports for power, water, and intelligence — three dependencies that a crew this wanted cannot afford.







⚡ Power Generation


Rime's Electro is the reason the Meridian has abundant power despite never docking at ports with recharging capabilities. He generates the ship's electricity — his Mink heritage producing a continuous current that Tidus's engineering routes through the ship's power grid. When the Meridian needs a surge — for weapons, for shields, for Claude's full sensor array — Rime just generates more. The ship runs on the heartbeat of a chinchilla who thinks being a living generator is a perfectly reasonable non-combat hobby. It is, arguably, the most important job on the ship, and Rime treats it with exactly the same casual disregard he treats everything that isn't fighting. He does it flawlessly.






💧 Water Harvesting


The Meridian never runs out of fresh water. This is Rime. His Jōki Jōki no Mi allows him to pull water vapour from the atmosphere — even in arid climates, there is always moisture for a Logia who is vapour. He condenses it into raw water, which Tidus then properly purifies through his water-manipulation abilities, and Petal treats through botanical filtration — a living water treatment process that produces water cleaner than anything a port could supply. The three of them worked out this chain during a drought crossing of the Calm Belt, and it has been standard procedure ever since. Rime finds it boring. He does it anyway, because the crew needs water, and Rime would cross an ocean of boredom for the people on this ship without a second thought.






📡 Radio Wave Relay


Rime's electromagnetic control extends beyond combat applications. By modulating his Electro output into specific frequencies, he acts as a living antenna array — pulling in radio waves, Den Den Mushi transmissions, Marine communications, and electromagnetic signals from across massive distances. This is how Claude accesses information being transmitted around the planet. Rime doesn't decode the signals himself — he simply channels them into Claude's processing architecture, serving as the raw receiver. The result: Claude has access to global communications without any external equipment. Rime is, functionally, the most advanced signals intelligence asset on the Grand Line, and he has absolutely no idea what most of the information he's channeling even means. He doesn't need to. That's Claude's job. Rime's job is to hear everything, and he hears everything.
















Self-portrait — chosen by Petal






🐥 Petal


Song of the Forest ・ Vegapunk Mink Experiment #041-A



A plump, fluffy chick — slightly larger than a normal chick — with soft golden-green down that shimmers faintly in sunlight, as though dusted with pollen. Her eyes are dark, sharp, and startlingly perceptive — the kind that make people feel, uncomfortably, that they've been understood completely. Petal inherited the full Mink-level intelligence, superhuman agility, and enhanced physical capabilities from the gene synthesis. What she did not inherit was Electro — because she has feathers, not fur, and Electro requires fur as a conductive medium. A chick with the speed, reflexes, and combat instincts of a Mink warrior produces something uniquely absurd and uniquely lethal.




Then she ate the Shoku Shoku no Mi, Model: Dryad — a Mythical Zoan with a lineage that stretches back to the creation of the Boin Archipelago itself — and everything got much more interesting.




Petal is quiet, watchful, deeply compassionate, and absolutely terrifying when she chooses to be. Where Rime fills rooms with noise, Petal fills them with presence. She speaks rarely and carefully, preferring soft chirps, tilted glances, and small gestures that communicate volumes to those who know her. She notices everything — not just because of her Observation Haki, but because she pays attention in a way most people don't. She remembers what you were worried about three weeks ago and checks on you with a flower left outside your door. She knows when someone is lying, when someone is hurting, and when someone needs to be left alone.




In combat, the quiet drops away and something ancient emerges. The Dryad fruit connects her to a lineage of nature spirits, and when Petal summons her Forms — when the moss bear rises, when the World Tree erupts from the earth — there is a stillness in her eyes that suggests she is channeling something far older than herself. She fights with the patience of a forest: slow to anger, impossible to exhaust, and devastating when the roots finally move. Opponents who mistake her gentleness for weakness discover that nature is the most patient killer in existence, and Petal is its instrument.











Species


Chick (Mink Hybrid)


Full Mink physical capability, no Electro






Sex


Female






Devil Fruit


Shoku Shoku no Mi, Model: Dryad


Plant-Plant Fruit ・ Mythical Zoan






Electro


Absent


Feathers cannot conduct Electro






Lifespan


Giant+ Class


Trace Giant DNA anomaly






Mink Traits


Full Physical


Superhuman agility, speed, reflexes, strength






Historical Link


Boin Archipelago


An ancient Dryad user created the living island






Rokushiki


Master


Apex-level mastery, full six techniques









Haki Profile


## Petal's Haki Mastery



Petal possesses all three forms of Haki, with her Observation reaching a level of mastery that few living beings have ever achieved. Her Armament and Conqueror's Haki are both formidable — refined, elegant, and devastatingly effective for a creature of her size.







Observation Haki

Emperor

Supreme Intent Perception




Among the most refined Observation Haki ever recorded. Reads intent itself — knows what opponents will do before they've consciously decided. Senses the emotional state of every living thing within range. Identifies individuals by the shape of their will from miles away.






Armament Haki

Elite Officer

Full Hardening + Emission




Proficient hardening and emission capable of reinforcing her small body and her botanical Forms. Particularly effective when channeled through the Treant's sword and shield, amplifying their striking power and defensive resilience.






Conqueror's Haki

Elite Officer

Refined Willpower Projection




A focused, elegant Conqueror's that manifests as an overwhelming aura of natural authority — like standing before a force of nature. Opponents feel their will wilt like flowers in drought. Particularly effective at dominating animal and beast-type opponents, who instinctively submit.









Martial Arts


## Rokushiki — Apex Mastery



Petal has achieved apex-level Rokushiki mastery — the highest tier of the six superhuman techniques, matching the proficiency of the World Government's most elite combat operatives. Her Mink-enhanced physique makes her Soru and Geppo terrifyingly fast, her Kami-e borders on precognitive (amplified by her Observation), and her Rankyaku — delivered through tiny but Mink-powered talon kicks — produces air slashes with cutting power that belies her size. Her Tekkai is proportionally the most impressive: a chick-sized body that, when hardened, can tank strikes from opponents a hundred times her size. She uses Shigan through her beak — a pecking strike at bullet velocity with Mink-level force behind it.





Mythical Zoan


## Shoku Shoku no Mi, Model: Dryad



The Plant-Plant Fruit, Model: Dryad is a Mythical Zoan that transforms the user into a dryad — a sentient nature spirit from ancient legend. The fruit grants mastery over plant life at a fundamental level: growth, shaping, animation, and the creation of living botanical constructs with their own rudimentary intelligence.




An ancient user of this fruit is believed to have created the Boin Archipelago — an entire living island ecosystem of predatory and symbiotic plant life — and is the originator of Pop Greens, the weaponized seed technology. In Petal's tiny wings, the fruit manifests as the ability to summon sentient plant-based Forms — autonomous botanical constructs that fight alongside her, each with distinct abilities and personalities.





Supreme Perception


## Observation Haki — World-Class



Petal possesses what may be the most refined Observation Haki currently active in the world — a level of perception so advanced it borders on omniscience within her range. She doesn't merely sense presence or predict movement; she reads intent itself. She knows what an opponent will do before they've consciously decided to do it. She feels the emotional state of every living thing around her. She can sense killing intent from miles away and identify specific individuals by the "shape" of their will.




Combined with her Mink-enhanced reflexes, this makes Petal nearly impossible to hit despite being a small chick. She moves before attacks are launched, dodges before trajectories form, and positions her Forms with precognitive accuracy. Her Observation is also the reason Ajay trusts her completely — nothing approaches the Hive Codex without Petal knowing first.





Botanical Summons


## Dryad Forms



Petal can summon twelve sentient plant-based constructs, each with distinct combat capabilities and rudimentary personalities. These Forms are not illusions or projections — they are living botanical entities animated by the Dryad fruit's mythical power. Each one requires concentration to maintain, and Petal can sustain up to three simultaneously at full power.








Form I — Defense

Guardian


▾






A giant, cuddly bear made entirely of dense moss and woven bark. Deceptively adorable — it waddles, tilts its head curiously, and makes soft rustling sounds. Then it punches with the force of an enhanced grizzly and shrugs off attacks that would fell warships. Exceptional defenses thanks to a moisture-saturated moss layer that resists flames and absorbs blunt impact. Its bark-layered core regenerates minor damage passively. The trio and Ajay's front-line protector, Petal's most frequently summoned Form, and the emotional heart of the group's dynamic. Guardian has been known to sit on enemies to pin them.











Form II — Offense

Sunflower


▾






A towering sunflower construct that tracks targets with its bloom head. The center of the flower gathers ambient solar energy and compresses it into a devastating focused beam — a botanical laser. Slow to recharge but each shot carries terrifying penetrating power. Can adjust beam width from precision lance to wide-angle scorching sweep.











Form III — Offense / Defense

Treant


▾






A sentient tree warrior — noble, disciplined, and combat-trained. Fights like a true paladin with honor-coded behavior and tactical intelligence. Wields a hardwood sword that can extend thorned vines on command for whip-strikes, entanglement, and disarming maneuvers. Carries a living bark shield that regenerates damage by regrowing bark layers and can sprout thorn spikes on impact to punish attackers. The most independently intelligent of all Forms — capable of complex tactical decisions, flanking maneuvers, and coordinated defense without Petal's direct input. Has been observed saluting Petal before battle.











Form IV — Support

Lotus Pool


▾






Not a creature but a terrain transformation. Petal grows a field of luminous lotuses across the ground that emit a regenerative mist. Allies standing within the lotus field experience accelerated wound closure, toxin neutralization, and stamina recovery. The lotuses also detect footfalls and relay position data to Petal's Observation Haki.











Form V — Offense / Zone Control

Briar Ankylos


▾






A heavy, armored ankylosaur construct built from deep plum-colored blackthorn and dark rosewood, with overlapping bark-armor plates in shades of burgundy and dark chocolate. Each plate is studded with paralytic thorns that glisten with amber sap like drops of honey. Patches of dark red roses grow stubbornly between the armor plates — beautiful and painful to touch. Its massive club-tail is a solid knot of hardened thornwood wrapped in dried crimson vines, capable of devastating sweeps that seed the ground with thorn-caltrops and shattered bark shrapnel. The Briar Ankylos does not chase. It advances — slow, steady, and unstoppable — and its tail demolishes everything in a wide arc. It is the most aggressive of Petal's Forms, not because it is fast, but because nothing has ever made it stop. Small white flowers bloom in the cracks of its armor, as if even this walking fortress cannot stop growing pretty things.











Form VI — Zone Control / Utility

Spore Cloud


▾






A floating mass of fungal spores animated into a drifting, amorphous entity. Releases different spore types on command: hallucinogenic, corrosive, adhesive, and soporific. Can split into multiple smaller clouds for wide-area coverage. Nearly impossible to destroy physically — dispersed spores simply reconverge.











Form VII — Offense / Zone Control

Nature Golem


▾






A hulking golem constructed from moss-covered boulders, packed earth, and living rock fused together by ancient roots and creeping vines. Ferns and wildflowers sprout from the cracks between its stone plates. Its fists are granite wrapped in thick ivy. Its eyes are two glowing amber crystals embedded deep in a craggy head of stacked rock overgrown with soft green moss. Mushrooms grow on its shoulders. Lichen patterns its chest like natural tattoos. The Nature Golem serves as Petal's long-range heavy bombardment platform — it tears chunks of itself free and hurls them across the battlefield with devastating accuracy, each boulder trailing roots and moss that explode into entangling vegetation on impact. When overwhelmed at close range, the Golem can shatter itself into a dozen smaller rock-golems — cute, round, mossy little boulder-creatures with the same amber crystal eyes — that swarm opponents with surprising coordination before reassembling into the full form. It is earth and plant merged into one slow, heavy, gentle giant — a walking hillside with a kind face and a terrifying throwing arm.











Form VIII — Offense / Zone Control

Rafflesia Triceratops


▾






A botanical Triceratops — the unmistakable three-horned silhouette, heavy frill, stocky four-legged stance — built entirely from fleshy rafflesia plant matter in deep crimson and pale cream, spotted and mottled like a poisonous toadstool. Its three horns are curved stems of hardened pitcher-plant material, hollow and dripping with sweet-smelling digestive nectar that dissolves anything organic on contact. The wide frill behind its head is a single enormous rafflesia bloom spread flat, concealing a toothed maw that opens when the head lowers — the creature charges frill-first into enemies and the frill snaps shut on whatever it catches. Candy-pink and warm gold bioluminescent lures dot the edges of the frill, attracting prey before the charge. It has a weirdly cute face — big round petal-pattern eyes and a stubby beak-mouth that looks like it's smiling. It is adorable and it eats things. The stench is considerable. Rime has opinions about this. The Rafflesia Triceratops does not care about Rime's opinions.











Form IX — Utility / Zone Control

Dandelion Sprites


▾






A group of tiny humanoid sprites made entirely of dandelion fluff — each one about the size of a hand, with round fluffy heads of white seeds, small bright eyes, and delicate wispy limbs. They float and drift through the air on their own fluff, trailing wisps of white-gold light and faint pink sparkles wherever they go. Individually, each Sprite can detonate its seed-head into a burst of thorns, a flash of pollen, or a small vine entanglement. Collectively, they fill the battlefield with motion, light, and coordinated harassment — some holding hands in chains, some tumbling playfully to distract, some clustering together in formations before dispersing in every direction at once. They look like the most harmless, adorable things Petal has ever created. They are simultaneously her widest-reaching reconnaissance network and her most underestimated weapon. The philosophical mirror of Ajay's own swarm doctrine — many small souls, one coordinated will.











Form X — Defense / Support

Briarcrest


▾






A botanical Parasaurolophus — the classic duck-billed dinosaur standing tall and graceful on powerful hind legs, with a long elegant neck and broad flat beak-mouth. Its body is made of living wood and woven vines in bright green with warm orange markings along its back and crest. The defining feature is the long, sweeping Parasaurolophus crest curving backward from its skull — smooth wood with orange-gold coloring that glows faintly from within, a natural resonance chamber that produces a deep, bone-shaking tone when it calls. Small wildflowers grow along the ridge of its back and down its neck like a floral mane. The Briarcrest's call strengthens allied resolve, enhances coordination between other Forms, and disorients enemies with sustained low-frequency vibration. Its body weeps a thin film of paralytic sap that punishes anything foolish enough to make contact. Where the Briar Ankylos advances, the Briarcrest holds — it plants its powerful legs and refuses to yield ground, its war-horn call rallying every Form within range. Elegant where the Ankylos is brutal, defensive where the Ankylos is aggressive — two botanical dinosaurs from the same garden, one built to push forward and one built to never be pushed back.











Form XI — Support / Healing

Willowmend


▾






A graceful weeping willow construct whose cascading fronds trail across the wounded like gentle fingers. Willowmend is Petal's most advanced healing Form — not a terrain transformation like Lotus Pool but an autonomous medic that moves across the battlefield, seeking out the injured and wrapping them in restorative vine-canopies. Its weeping sap is a powerful analgesic and regenerative compound that accelerates recovery beyond what Lotus Pool can achieve. Where Lotus Pool provides passive area healing, Willowmend provides active targeted care — finding the most critical wounds and concentrating Dryad life-force where it's needed most. The construct moves with a mournful beauty, its fronds swaying as though in grief for every wound it finds. It has been known to cradle injured allies in its branches, humming a frequency that Petal says she doesn't consciously choose.











Form XII — Final Form

World Tree — Yggdrasil


▾






Petal's ultimate summoning — a power that echoes the ancient Dryad who shaped the Boin Archipelago itself. A colossal tree erupts from the earth, its canopy blotting out the sky, its roots diving deep enough to anchor into bedrock. The World Tree is a living ecosystem — it generates all other Forms simultaneously from its branches and roots, sustains a permanent Lotus Pool healing field beneath its canopy, channels Petal's Observation Haki through its root network to sense everything within a vast radius, and produces Pop Green seeds that scatter across the battlefield as deployable munitions. For as long as Yggdrasil stands, Petal operates at the absolute peak of her power — twelve Forms active, full healing coverage, total sensory dominance. The cost is total concentration — she can do nothing else while maintaining it, and its destruction leaves her completely drained. The World Tree has been summoned only twice. Both times, the battlefield was unrecognizable afterward.











THE DRYAD FORMS — THE GARDEN'S VANGUARD







THE DRYAD FORMS — THE ANCIENT GROVE








Resonance Art


## Sproutling Technique







Resonance Art — Sproutlings

Tsuyu's Nursery — Meadow of Rebirth


▾






Leas-lann Dhriùchd — Cluain an Ath-bhreith


Petal calls the Sproutlings to her and channels her Mythical Zoan life-force directly through them — amplifying their trace Dryad resonance to full output. The Sproutlings scatter across the ground and root themselves, each one blooming into a miniature garden node. Within seconds, the battlefield transforms: grass erupts from bare earth, wildflowers burst open, vines thread between the nodes, and a living meadow unfurls across the entire engagement zone. Every plant within the meadow is connected to Petal's Observation — she feels every footstep, every disturbance, every breath taken by anyone standing on living ground. The meadow also carries passive regenerative properties — allies standing in the flowers heal slowly, while Petal can concentrate Dryad energy through any node to create localized healing bursts. Tsuyu stands at the center of her nursery, leaf umbrella held high, crying tears of joy. The flowers that grow where her tears fall are the ones that heal the fastest.








Personal Combat


## Petal's Combat Doctrine



Even without her Forms, Petal is a formidable fighter. Her Mink-enhanced physicality in a chick's body produces a uniquely bizarre and effective combat style — impossibly fast pecking strikes that hit with force far beyond her size, wing-buffets that generate concussive gusts, talon kicks with Mink-level power, and aerial agility that makes her nearly untouchable. Her supreme Observation reads every attack before it begins, and her apex Rokushiki gives her the tools to respond with surgical precision.




In close combat, Petal favors a Geppo-dominant aerial style — air-stepping at extreme speed while delivering Rankyaku air slashes from her talons and Shigan pecking strikes with her beak. She chains these into rapid Soru-burst passes that leave opponents swatting at afterimages. When cornered, her Tekkai allows her to tank hits that should obliterate a creature her size, and she counters with Conqueror's-infused wing slaps that carry spiritual weight far beyond their physical force.




Her preferred tactic is to summon two to three Forms for heavy engagement while she herself provides Observation-guided coordination and opportunistic strikes from angles her opponents never expect — because no one watches the tiny chick when there's a moss bear and a tree knight charging at them. In group engagements alongside Rime, Tidus, and Ajay, Petal serves as the battlefield coordinator for the entire trio — her supreme Observation feeds positional data to all three companions and Ajay simultaneously. She calls targets, redirects Forms mid-combat, and delivers precision finishing blows herself when openings appear. Her Dryad powers also let her seed the battlefield with dormant plant constructs during a fight, creating traps and resources that activate on her command minutes later.





Petal's Garden


## Original Pop Greens



Petal has developed her own Pop Greens through her Mythical Zoan Dryad powers. She cross-breeds plants in the Sky Garden, infusing them with trace Dryad life-force. Her Pop Greens are unique because they're partially alive with Dryad resonance — they respond to her Observation Haki. She tends the garden at dawn with Sprocket and the Sproutlings, talking to every plant. They grow faster when she does. She's the crew's primary food source through her garden — another quiet but essential role she fills without ever asking for recognition.







Moonbloom Seeds


Grow into bioluminescent flowers that provide light and passive healing to anyone standing near them. She lines the ship corridors with them. The soft blue-white glow is the reason the Meridian's interior always feels gentle, even at night.






Thornwall Pods


Burst into instant thorn barriers reinforced by her Dryad energy. Harder than steel, covered in paralytic thorns. Can be shaped to protect specific areas. The thorns dissolve after an hour, leaving no permanent damage to the environment.






Pollen Veil Seeds


Release a massive cloud of golden pollen that enhances allied Observation Haki while disorienting enemies. The crew fights sharper inside the veil. Enemies fight blind. The asymmetry is devastating.






Root Snare Grenades


On impact, roots erupt and entangle everything in a 10-meter radius. The roots can be made gentle (for rescue operations, pulling people from rubble) or crushing (for combat restraint). Petal controls the intensity through intent.






Dreamleaf Herbs


Medicinal Pop Greens that accelerate healing and induce restful sleep. She grows these specifically for Ajay's post-「Ajay」recovery, when his body is exhausted and his mind won't stop running. The scent is lavender and rain.






Skyblossom Spores


Flower-seeds that grow in midair, creating floating flower platforms the crew can use as stepping stones. In combat, they provide aerial mobility. In peace, Petal makes flower-paths through the clouds for the crew to walk on. It is, by universal consensus, the most beautiful thing anyone has ever seen.






Sunfruit Vine


Produces small, incredibly nutritious fruit. One fruit equals one full meal. Tastes like honey and citrus. The vine grows throughout the galley and produces year-round. Tidus' breakfasts frequently feature Sunfruit preserves.






Whispergrass


A grass that transmits sound across distances. Petal plants patches around the ship as a secondary communication network. If Claude's Mote system ever goes down, the Whispergrass network can carry messages. The crew sometimes uses it to send each other quiet words they don't want to say aloud. Rime has been caught whispering "goodnight" into a Whispergrass patch near Petal's roost. He denies this.
















Self-portrait — chosen by Tidus






🐻‍❄️ Tidus


Heart of the Ocean ・ Vegapunk Experiment #063-C



An adorably chubby polar bear–sea otter hybrid — round-bellied, soft, with dense white-cream fur that carries the water-repellent quality of sea otter pelage over the thick insulating bulk of a polar bear's coat. When wet, his fur takes on an iridescent sheen from the bio-film that coats his body — a gift of his clownfish DNA. Blue eyes, warm and gentle. Slightly oversized paws with subtle webbing between the toes — otter influence that makes him a devastatingly graceful swimmer despite looking like a stuffed animal that wandered into a battlefield. His tail is more otter than bear — flat, broad, useful for swimming and balance. His Pacifista modifications are entirely internal — enhanced skeletal structure, embedded power systems, internal weapon hardpoints — and not visually obvious from outside. Another reason the researchers classified the experiment as a failure. Tidus is the result of Vegapunk attempting to create a fearsome aquatic predator hybrid — polar bear strength, sea otter agility, Fishman DNA, and Pacifista weapons integration. What they got was an overwhelmingly adorable creature whose appearance is the opposite of intimidating.




The World Government wanted a war machine. They got a gentle, brilliant, absurdly lovable bear-otter who happens to possess one of the most powerful wills in recorded history. They threw him away. They were catastrophically wrong.




Tidus is kind in the way that tides are relentless — not as a choice, but as a fundamental property of what he is. He speaks softly, thinks carefully, and treats every living thing he encounters with a gentleness that borders on reverence. He apologizes to furniture he bumps into. He names every homie that finds him and builds them tiny houses nestled in the thick fur on his back. He has never once raised his voice in anger. When asked how he can possess Conqueror's Haki — the embodiment of the will to rule — while being the least aggressive creature anyone has ever met, Tidus simply says: "I don't want to rule anyone. I just want everyone to be safe. Maybe that's enough."




It is more than enough. His Conqueror's Haki is among the strongest ever recorded — and it manifests not as domination but as sanctuary. He is also a genius-level engineer who built the Meridian from salvage and ingenuity and solves mechanical problems the way other people breathe. He is the crew's engineer, medic, craftsman, and emotional anchor — the foundation everything else is built on. Where Ajay designs war, Tidus designs sanctuary. And between the two of them, everyone they love is covered.




His name is his own. As a baby in Vegapunk's lab, he could hear the tides through the walls. The laboratory was coastal — built into cliffs above the sea. At night, when the machines quieted and the researchers went home, the rhythmic pulse of ocean against stone was the only sound that wasn't clinical. It was the only thing in his world that felt like it was there FOR him, not STUDYING him. When he first tried to speak — early, before the researchers expected language — he attempted to say "tides." The word that meant safety. Comfort. The thing that never stopped coming back, no matter how bad the day had been. What came out, in his small, earnest voice, was: "Tidus." He never corrected it. He didn't know it was wrong. The researchers logged it as a designation error — "Subject T-063 self-identifies as 'Tidus,' catalogued as speech development anomaly." They never realized it was a love letter to the only friend he had.




He has a favourite pebble. A smooth, round stone — grey-blue, sea-worn, perfectly ordinary. He keeps it with him always, tucked into the curve of his paw where it fits as though it grew there. He holds it when he thinks. He holds it when he builds. He holds it when the ship is quiet and everyone else is asleep and the ocean is dark beneath them. No one knows where it came from. Tidus has never explained, and no one has ever asked, because some things don't need explaining. They just need holding.




Personality expressions: When content, he chuffs softly and rolls onto his back, eyes closing — otter grooming behaviour at its most peaceful. When excited about engineering, his paws patter rapidly on whatever surface is nearest, tail smacking the ground in bear-cub excitement. In protective mode, he rises to full height on hind legs — a polar bear's threat display from the gentlest creature alive — with a low rumbling purr and fur bristling slightly. When alarmed, a sharp bark; teeth bared (formidable polar bear teeth), positioning himself between threat and whoever needs protecting. When happy, a cooing chirp — sea otter influence — and sometimes he floats on his back in water and chirps, which is his most peaceful state. When apologetic (which is often, especially after hitting opponents), he ducks his head, covers his muzzle with both paws, and makes small whimpering sounds. He does this after every fight. Every single one.











Species

Haki Profile


## Tidus' Haki Mastery



Tidus' Haki profile is one of the most unusual in the world. He has no interest in fighting, no bloodlust, no ambition for conquest. He is, by every measure, a gentle soul. And yet his Conqueror's Haki is among the most powerful ever manifested — a paradox that confounds everyone who encounters it. His will is not the will to dominate. It is the will to protect absolutely, and that conviction is so total, so unwavering, that reality itself yields to it.







Conqueror's Haki

Supreme Ruler

Advanced Coating — Full Infusion Mastery




Among the most powerful Conqueror's Haki ever recorded — superior to "Red-Haired" Shanks'. During the Egghead engagement, Tidus' domain-level Conqueror's pushed Shanks' will back for the first time in the Emperor's adult life, forcing his domain to contract. Has achieved full advanced coating — can imbue Conqueror's into his defenses at extreme concentration, release it through Fishman Karate techniques, and project sovereign will through everything he creates. His Conqueror's does not oppress — it shelters. It is the will to protect absolutely, and that conviction is so total that even an Emperor's authority becomes a visitor within Tidus' jurisdiction, not a resident.






Armament Haki

Commander

Advanced Hardening + Emission




Full hardening and emission mastery. Layered into his dense fur, Pacifista-reinforced skeletal structure, and bio-film coating for devastating defensive reinforcement. Channeled through Fishman Karate water-manipulation techniques for projected strikes that carry Haki through the film-water medium.






Observation Haki

Commander

Advanced Perception + Emotional Resonance




Highly refined situational awareness with a unique emotional-resonance quality — Tidus doesn't just sense hostile intent, he feels the emotional state of those around him with profound clarity. This makes him an exceptional medic, counselor, and protector who knows when someone is in pain before they show it.









Fishman Heritage


## Fishman DNA — Clownfish + Fancy Goldfish



Among the DNA spliced into Experiment #063-C, the Fishman components were selected for aquatic adaptation and underwater combat capability. What Vegapunk got was something far more interesting than a weapon.




Clownfish (Amphiprioninae): Tidus produces a protective bio-film coating that grants immunity to contact toxins, acids, and chemical attacks — the real-world superpower of clownfish, who produce a film membrane that makes them immune to anemone stings. Tidus can extend this bio-film to coat allies, creating incredible synergy with Rime's acidic vapor — he is naturally immune to it and can protect others walking through acid clouds. The clownfish DNA also gives him a fierce territorial instinct: despite being gentle, he gets aggressive for everyone he considers family. His symbiotic nature means he doesn't just protect — he enables. His presence makes everyone around him stronger.




Fancy Goldfish (Ranchu/Oranda type): Goldfish survive conditions that kill most fish — temperature extremes, low oxygen, poor water quality. The goldfish DNA is why Tidus is indestructible despite looking like a plush toy. It contributes to his adorable roundness, enhances his bio-film production, and embodies his core aesthetic: looks harmless, survives everything. Peak Tidus energy.




Combat Sound Signature: A deep resonant POP — the sound of film-coated water bursting under enormous pressure. Think of a giant bubble popping with concussive force. When the crew hears that sound, they know Tidus just hit something with intent.






Martial Art


## Fishman Karate — Grandmaster (Adapted)



Tidus' Fishman Karate has reached grandmaster-level proficiency — adapted entirely for his bear-otter anatomy. His fighting school is built around film-membrane water manipulation — thick, viscous water constructs that aren't just walls but sticky, clinging, shock-absorbing membranes. His bio-film coating from the clownfish DNA gives his water constructs an iridescent quality and a texture that no other Fishman Karate practitioner can replicate — elastic, adhesive, shimmering.




His primary striking technique is the Paw-Clap Shockwave — Tidus claps his heavy polar bear paws together, compressing film-coated water between them. The resulting pop sends a viscous shockwave that clings to whatever it hits. Unlike a sharp impact that pushes through, this shockwave wraps around the target and continues delivering internal pressure damage after the initial hit. Less sharp crack, more deep resonant pop. His blocks create sticky water barriers that grab and slow incoming attacks. His strikes deliver force through a film-water medium that adheres to the target. Underwater, he's even more devastating — polar bear swimming power plus otter agility plus Fishman water control.






Martial Arts


## Rokushiki — Advanced Operative



Tidus has learned Rokushiki to an advanced operative level — the tier associated with the World Government's most elite intelligence agents. Each art adapted for his polar bear-otter body:




Tekkai: Devastating given his natural bulk — dense polar bear muscle, thick fur, Pacifista skeletal reinforcement, and bio-film coating. Combined with Conqueror's Haki, he becomes a fortress that not even cannon fire can scratch. The most natural Rokushiki for Tidus — he was built to take hits.


Geppo: Powerful hind-leg bursts — bears have incredible leg strength. Less rapid than Rime's, but each air-step covers more distance and hits harder. The air beneath him compresses visibly. His Geppo looks like a polar bear bounding through the sky.


Soru: Forward charges rather than lateral movement. A polar bear at Soru-speed is a freight train of fluff. The ground cracks. The air displaces. And then the softest possible collision.


Shigan: Paw-thrust variant. Less pointed than finger-pistol — bears have blunt paws — more about channeling massive force through a single point. Also: his bite. Polar bear jaw strength is extraordinary.


Rankyaku: Comes from tail sweeps — his otter tail is flat and powerful — or massive paw swipes that compress air into cutting waves. The waves are thick and visible, like water blades.


Kami-e: The most surprising one. Tidus looks like he shouldn't be able to dodge anything. But sea otters are incredibly fluid in water, and that translates to a deceptive body fluidity on land. His Kami-e is a gentle, swaying evasion — opponents' attacks slide off him like he's made of water.






Blackbeard Gene Heritage


## The Hidden Fruits



Vegapunk was attempting to replicate Blackbeard's anomalous multi-fruit capacity — the only confirmed case of a single body hosting both a Paramecia and a Logia simultaneously. His approach: use Blackbeard's Lineage Factor as the genetic substrate in Experiment #063-C, then administer partial Green Blood extracts of two deliberately weak, inferior Devil Fruits — theorizing that antagonistic interference between two weak fruits would be proportionally weaker, allowing the Blackbeard genes to stabilize coexistence. If the body could host two fruits that barely wanted to exist, perhaps it could host two fruits that fully wanted to exist later. The cub was a proof of concept. A stepping stone. A disposable test.




The two fruits chosen: 1. Yuki Yuki no Mi — Snow-Snow Fruit (Logia) — a partial extract, significantly weaker than the full fruit Monet once wielded. 2. Kawa Kawa no Mi (カワカワの実) — Cute-Cute Fruit (Paramecia) — an inferior derivative of the Mero Mero no Mi. Where Hancock's fruit petrifies through desire and attraction, this lesser version operates through the cuteness response — the involuntary biological softening that occurs when a creature perceives something overwhelmingly adorable. Vegapunk selected it specifically because it was weak, because it was laughable, because no serious researcher would waste resources studying a fruit whose primary combat application was "being cute." It was perfect fodder for an experiment no one expected to succeed.




The experiment subject: a polar bear–sea otter hybrid cub designated Experiment #063-C. Already spliced with clownfish and fancy goldfish DNA for bio-film generation. Already carrying Fishman DNA for aquatic adaptation. Already modified with Pacifista cybernetics for structural reinforcement. Already considered a "disappointing" specimen due to his adorable appearance — too round, too soft, too gentle, too much like a toy and not enough like a weapon. The extracts were administered. Vegapunk ran tests. The cub could still swim. Showed no signs of sea curse. Did not produce snow in any recorded trial. Did not exhibit charm effects under any measured condition. By every metric: unchanged. Vegapunk concluded the result was sub-threshold — a failed experiment — and moved on to more promising subjects.




The abilities took. Both of them. Fully.




Tidus never used them in the laboratory because he understood, with the quiet intelligence everyone mistook for simplicity, exactly what would happen if he did. If he produced snow, they would run more tests. If the Cute-Cute Fruit activated, they would weaponize it. If they knew the Blackbeard experiment had worked — even partially — they would never stop. They would dissect the success. They would replicate it. They would take him apart to find out why, and they would build something terrible from the answer.




So the gentlest creature in the laboratory looked at the scientists with his big blue eyes, and he did nothing, and they wrote "FAILED" in the margins, and they moved on. And the cub who could make winter and could make the world go soft kept those gifts hidden inside his chest like secrets held for people who deserved them. He didn't use them until he had a family worth protecting. And then he awakened both.




Sea Curse Immunity




Not by design. Not by theory. By accident — the kindest kind. The sheer density of overlapping genetic modifications — polar bear, sea otter, Fishman splicing, Pacifista cybernetics, Blackbeard Lineage Factor, two simultaneous partial Green Blood infusions — produced a cascade of unpredictable compensatory mutations. One of those mutations rendered him immune to the sea curse. It was not engineered. It was a random beneficial anomaly — the biological equivalent of a rounding error that happened to round in the kindest possible direction. The universe looked at the most gentle, most harmless creature in the laboratory, and it gave him a gift that none of the fearsome specimens received: the ability to carry Devil Fruit power and still swim.




Vegapunk never identified the mutation because he never looked for it. He assumed the fruits didn't take. Why would he investigate the immune profile of a failed experiment?



### Yuki Yuki no Mi — Snow-Snow Fruit (Logia Partial)



Tidus produces powdered snow from his body — not ice directly. He makes snow. The distinction matters. Snow is soft. Snow is gentle. Snow muffles sound and blankets the world in quiet. It is the most Tidus substance imaginable, and the fact that his Logia partial generates this rather than razor-sharp ice or freezing mist says everything about the nature of the creature who hosts it.




He can compact snow using Haki infusion to create ice-hard structures — snow compressed by sovereign will until it forgets it was ever soft. Conqueror's-infused snowpack achieves the density of cannonball iron while retaining the crystalline beauty of fresh powder. The result is building material that looks like winter and hits like warship armor.




The partial extract grants productive and manipulative aspects of the Logia without the transformation. No Logia intangibility — Tidus cannot become snow, cannot let attacks pass through him, cannot dissipate and reform. He is fully solid at all times. His own cold does affect his body — but polar bear–sea otter physiology provides more than sufficient compensation. Dense fur, subcutaneous fat, cold-adapted circulatory systems. The cold is his element. It just can't make him immaterial.




Hypothermic contact: Tidus can lower the body temperature of anyone he physically holds, inducing drowsiness and eventual sleep. Given that his combat doctrine involves embracing and shielding, and given that enemies find him extraordinarily hard to take seriously until he's already hugging them — this is deceptively dangerous. His hugs can put people to sleep. The warmth of his fur masks the cold creeping through contact until the target's eyelids are already heavy.




Snow can be channeled through his enormous paws, through the shell shovel, or projected at range. Combined with Fishman Karate bio-film manipulation: film-coated snow structures — compacted snow wrapped in shock-absorbent bio-film that flexes on impact instead of shattering. Combined with the shell shovel's coral: frozen reef structures — living coral threaded through Haki-compacted snow, the reef alive beneath the frost, bioluminescence glowing through ice like lanterns under a frozen sea.




Tidus provides the cold element for the crew's combination attacks — the frozen landscape, the temperature drop, the crystalline constructs. He is the crew's winter-maker. The ice is distinctly his — blue-white with the iridescent sheen of bio-film, like frozen moonlight through soap bubbles.



### Kawa Kawa no Mi — Cute-Cute Fruit (Paramecia)



Passive Effect — Pacification Aura: The Kawa Kawa no Mi generates a persistent passive aura that saps hostile intent from those who perceive Tidus. This is not mind control — it is the amplification of the natural cuteness response pushed to supernatural levels. The involuntary biological reaction that makes mammals soften when they see something small, round, and helpless — except Tidus is neither small nor helpless, and the fruit doesn't care. It broadcasts "protect this creature" directly into the hindbrain of every hostile entity in range.




Effects: aggression dulls. Attacks lose force. Guards drop. Observation Haki targeting stutters — reads Tidus as "non-threat" even when the conscious mind knows better. The effect scales with proximity. Strong-willed fighters can push through, but still experience a momentary lapse — a half-second where the sword feels heavy and the target looks like something you should be protecting, not attacking. That half-second is all Tidus needs. Allies are unaffected — the fruit only targets hostile intent.




Active Effect — Softening Projection: Tidus can project waves of physical softening. Stone becomes clay. Steel becomes pliable. Armor loses rigidity. Weapons passing through the field hit with reduced structural integrity — not slower, but softer, the materials themselves losing the hardness required to do damage. Against living targets: limbs feel heavy, joints loose, muscle tension relaxes involuntarily — the body's structural support temporarily softens as though every cell has decided to relax whether or not the mind agrees. Not painful. Not permanent. Just... soft.




Synergy with Conqueror's Haki: The combination is devastating. Conqueror's says "you cannot break through." Cute-Cute says "you don't want to." Weapons and armor are physically weakened by softening. Will is pressed by sovereign authority. And behind all of that: a polar bear–sea otter hybrid with Supreme Ruler-tier Haki, Grandmaster Fishman Karate, Pacifista internals, film-coated barriers, snow powers, coral architecture, and the unshakeable conviction that nobody gets hurt while he's here.




The Paradox: The very trait that made Vegapunk classify Experiment #063-C as a failure is the trait that makes Tidus nearly undefeatable in his role. The cuteness was not a defect — it was the delivery system. The experiment didn't fail. It succeeded beyond anything the scientist imagined. He just couldn't see it because he was looking for a weapon, and what he made was a shelter.






Weapon — Devil Fruit-Infused


## The Shell Shovel — Sango Sango no Mi (Reef-Reef Fruit)



Tidus carries a shell-shaped shovel-staff — a blue staff topped with an iridescent shell fan. This is both his building tool and his weapon, the instrument through which he constructs and defends and creates.




The shell shovel has eaten the Sango Sango no Mi — the Reef-Reef Fruit, a Zoan-type Devil Fruit. Corals are living creatures — colonial animals — making this a Zoan that allows the weapon to "become" a living coral reef entity. The fruit was fed directly to the weapon, in the same tradition as Spandam's Funkfreed or Lassoo the dog-bazooka. Tidus carries three Devil Fruit abilities — two hidden partial extracts within his body (the Yuki Yuki no Mi and Kawa Kawa no Mi) and the Sango Sango no Mi housed in his shell shovel weapon. The coral power comes from the weapon. The snow and the gentleness come from within.




In its base form, the shell fan can grow, branch, and reshape into coral structures — extending coral walls, creating barriers, growing reef architecture with the precision of an engineer's mind. In full transformation, the staff becomes a living reef creature that Tidus wields, rides, and directs — an extension of his building instinct made animate.




Seawater protection: The weapon has standard Devil Fruit weakness to seawater. Tidus protects it using his powerful Haki coating and Fishman Karate water manipulation — he maintains a bio-film barrier around the weapon when submerged, shielding it from direct seawater contact. This requires active concentration, adding a tactical dimension: he can't be reckless underwater with the weapon out.






Weapon Art


## Coral Techniques — Living Architecture



Through the shell shovel's Zoan power, Tidus can generate and manipulate living coral structures — crystalline formations of compressed minerals and bioactive water that grow from the weapon at extraordinary speed. The coral is not merely stone; it pulses with bioluminescence, responds to his will, and carries the same iridescent bubble-lattice pattern as his water barriers. When he channels the shovel's power, waves of water threaded with blooming coral formations cascade outward, hardening into architectural structures within seconds.




Tidus can grow coral walls, bridges, pillars, enclosures, and intricate frameworks with a precision that reflects his engineering mind. The coral absorbs and redistributes kinetic energy, making it exceptionally resilient. When infused with his Conqueror's Haki, coral structures become nearly indestructible — and when he no longer needs them, they dissolve harmlessly into sea-minerals, leaving no trace. Where his Fishman Karate controls water and his Sub-Arctic Art defends with fur and will, his Coral Techniques build — the constructor's art of a creature born to make shelter.






Supreme Will


## Conqueror's Haki — The Fluffy Doctrine



Tidus' Conqueror's Haki manifests not as a wall but as an inescapable embrace. His sovereign will doesn't project outward as domination — it envelops. It wraps around everything he's chosen to protect like a polar bear curling around its cubs. His will says: "Nothing touches what I love. Not because I am hard. Because I am WARM, and the warmth goes all the way down, and there is more warmth than you have cold."




A polar bear's body is already formidable: dense muscle hidden under soft fur, insulated against every extreme, built to endure. Layer Pacifista reinforcement over that. Layer Tekkai over that. Layer the strongest Conqueror's Haki in the non-human world over that. By combining Fishman Karate water manipulation, advanced Conqueror's coating, Pacifista structural integrity, and his natural bulk, Tidus can generate layered defensive barriers and forcefields that have withstood attacks from some of the New World's most powerful combatants.




His forcefields are compressed water structures infused with Conqueror's Haki, shaped and held in place by Fishman Karate mastery. They shimmer like soap bubbles — and this is not metaphor. His bio-film from the clownfish DNA gives his water constructs their iridescent quality. Each barrier is a lattice of pressurized film-bubbles carrying the spiritual density of a king's will. When struck, the attacker doesn't just hit physical resistance — they hit the full weight of Tidus' conviction to protect, and that conviction does not yield.





Named Techniques


## Tidus' Signature Moves





Sub-Arctic Art

Embrace of the Sovereign


▾






Kyokuchi-jutsu: Iwi Hao o ke Aliʻi


Tidus hunkers low — the way polar bears plant themselves against the Arctic wind, immovable and absolute — body angled forward, hind legs braced against the ground, forepaws wrapped around himself the way bears protect their vital organs. He channels maximum Conqueror's Haki into every layer of his defense simultaneously — dense fur bristling with sovereign will, Pacifista reinforcement hardened to maximum, Tekkai layered over that, bio-film barrier enveloping the exterior, and a compressed water shell around the whole formation. The result is a defensive state so absolute that attacks bounce off, shatter, or simply fail to register. Even advanced Haki users find their strikes blunted against a will that has decided, with total conviction, that nothing will pass. Tidus has held this state under sustained assault from multiple New World-class combatants without taking damage. His fur glows faintly with a warm golden light — his Conqueror's manifesting not as intimidation, but as sanctuary. Anyone trying to reach what's behind him has to go through the softest, most indestructible wall in the New World.









Fishman Karate

Tide Wall — Sovereign Barrier


▾






Gyojin Karate: Pā Kai — Pā Kiaʻi Aliʻi


Tidus draws ambient moisture from the air and compresses it into a shimmering, curved barrier wall at range — the surface rippling with the distinctive bubble-lattice pattern that marks all of his water constructs, each bubble a tiny pressurized sphere of his Conqueror's will. The water is then infused with Conqueror's Haki at extreme density, transforming it from a physical water-wall into a spiritual fortification. The barrier can be placed around allies, across doorways, or in front of incoming attacks. It absorbs and disperses kinetic energy, nullifies projectiles, and causes Haki-less attacks to simply stop dead. Multiple barriers can be layered for compound defense. Tidus' primary tool for protecting his friends — and the technique he uses most often.









Fishman Karate

Paw-Clap Shockwave — Depth Charge


▾






Gyojin Karate: Lima Maiao — Pōhaku Hohonu


Tidus' primary offensive Fishman Karate technique. He claps his heavy polar bear paws together, compressing film-coated water between them — the bio-film from his clownfish DNA giving the water a thick, viscous quality that holds under immense pressure before bursting.
